column1: Comprehensive Table of Biblical Characters with Controversies

column6: Notable Drama/Controversy
Fall of mankind, disobedience of God's command

One of only two humans (with Elijah) taken to heaven without dying

Nearly sacrificed by Abraham, deceived by Jacob and Rebekah

Sold birthright for soup, married Hittite women
Deceived father for blessing, deceived by Laban, family dysfunction
Deceived Jacob repeatedly, switched brides, changed wages
Unwanted in marriage, rivalry with sister

Raped by Shechem, leading to massacre by brothers
Massacred Shechemites in revenge for Dinah
Slept with father's concubine Bilhah
Slept with daughter-in-law Tamar (unknowingly)
Sold into slavery by jealous brothers
Falsely accused Joseph of assault after he rejected her advances
Killed Egyptian, struck rock in anger

Criticized Moses' Cushite wife, temporarily struck with leprosy
Led rebellion against Moses, swallowed by earth
Attempted to curse Israel, later advised seduction strategy

Adultery with David while married to Uriah
Raped his half-sister Tamar
Murdered Amnon, rebelled against David, slept with father's concubines
700 wives & 300 concubines, idolatry in later life
Harsh rule caused kingdom division
Established golden calf worship
Promoted Baal worship, murdered prophets, framed Naboth
Married Jezebel, promoted idolatry, coveted Naboth's vineyard
Slaughtered 450 prophets of Baal at Mt. Carmel
Cursed mocking youths who were mauled by bears
Murdered royal family to seize throne
Violent purge of Ahab's family and Baal worshippers
Fled God's call, angry at Nineveh's repentance
Commanded to marry promiscuous woman as object lesson
Struck with leprosy for usurping priestly role
Sacrificed his son in fire, altered temple worship
Walked naked and barefoot for three years as sign

column4: Most evil Judean king, late repentance
column6: Child sacrifice, extreme idolatry, filled Jerusalem with blood
Discovered lost Torah, reformed worship
Persecuted, imprisoned in cistern

Destroyed Jerusalem, temporary insanity
Thrown into lions' den for praying
Thrown into fiery furnace
"Writing on the wall," killed same night

Confronted nobles exploiting poor

Previously persecuted Christians
